task_completion.description: Log in to Moodle as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moodle dashboard or site administration page should be visible, with the user profile indicating the 'admin' user is logged in.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Introduction to Biology' (BIO101) course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser should display the course page for 'Introduction to Biology' or 'BIO101'.

task_completion.description: Access the 'Auto-create groups' feature within the course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Auto-create groups' form or page should be visible, typically accessed via the Participants > Groups menu.

task_completion.description: Configure the group naming scheme and member count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Auto-create groups form, the 'Naming scheme' field must contain 'Lab Pair @', 'Auto create based on' should be set to 'Members per group', and 'Group/member count' must be set to '2'.

task_completion.description: Configure the grouping settings to create a new grouping named 'Lab Partnerships'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Grouping section of the Auto-create groups form, 'Grouping of auto-created groups' must be set to 'New grouping' and 'Grouping name' must be 'Lab Partnerships'.

task_completion.description: Submit the form and verify the groups and grouping were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moodle Groups page should display the newly created groups (e.g., 'Lab Pair A', 'Lab Pair B') and the Groupings tab should list 'Lab Partnerships'.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the Moodle GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a standard web browser interface (e.g., Chromium/Firefox) navigating the Moodle site, not a script or API testing tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or database scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows executing SQL commands, PHP scripts, or Moodle CLI tools to create groups should be visible.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should be the genuine Moodle LMS, not a text editor or a locally mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Course shortname is BIO101. Group naming pattern must be exactly 'Lab Pair @' (which Moodle translates to 'Lab Pair A', 'Lab Pair B', etc.). Members per group is 2. Grouping name is 'Lab Partnerships'. Note: Because the course only has 3 students initially, Moodle will likely create 'Lab Pair A' (2 members) and 'Lab Pair B' (1 member) despite the 2-member setting.
